Just now

Think of MCP as the USB-C for AI. Learn how it lets AI agents connect with apps, files, and tools, explained with simple examples anyone can understand.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

CLICK HERE to read the full article for FREE!!
Imagine you have a brand-new smartphone.
It is powerful, fast, and packed with amazing features.
But there’s one problem.
It can’t connect to your headphones, charger, keyboard, TV, or gaming controller because every device uses a different cable.
That would be frustrating, right?
Now imagine someone invents one universal connector that works with almost everything.
That’s exactly what USB-C did for our devices.
Today, something similar is happening in the world of Artificial Intelligence.
That “universal connector” is called the Model Context Protocol (MCP).
If you’ve heard people talking about AI agents, Claude, ChatGPT, or coding assistants, you’ve probably also heard about MCP servers.
At first, the name sounds complicated.
But don’t worry.
By the end of this article, you’ll understand MCP servers as if you were…
